article_meta.title: Former President Donald Trump speaks at a campaign rally on April 27, 2023, in Manchester, New Hampshire

reasoning: The summary maintains a neutral tone, attributes evaluative conclusions to the article, and avoids adopting loaded or sensational language. One mildly strong phrase (“completely unsubstantiated”) aligns with the article’s evidence-based conclusion and is caveated. Perspectives from both anti- and pro-immigration experts, CNN, FactCheck.org, and the Trump campaign are included.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs like “claimed,” “cites,” and “stated”; avoids charged verbs.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: States lack of evidence with attribution to CNN and FactCheck.org; “completely unsubstantiated” is supported by reported searches.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Evaluative judgment is attributed to the article’s conclusion and evidence.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No assertions about motives or thoughts.
custom_reasoning.slant: Represents both Trump’s claims and multiple fact-checking/expert responses; includes caveat about proving absence.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Covers key perspectives (Trump campaign, anti- and pro-immigration experts, CNN, FactCheck.org); minor omission of non-responsive super PAC is negligible.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Mostly neutral; “completely unsubstantiated” is the only mildly strong phrase.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Critical points mirror the article; includes mitigating note about proof of absence.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats experts and organizations as sources without undue deference over others.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More measured than the opinion source; avoids loaded framing.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Includes all major perspectives present in the article with proportional weight.
